items.title: Amazon may launch a marketplace where media sites can sell their content to AI companies
items.description: A new report claims the e-commerce giant is looking to create a pipeline of licensable content between media publishers and AI companies.

items.title: Stock Market Today, Feb. 10: Amazon Doubles Down on AI as AWS Drives $200 Billion Capex Push
items.description: Amazon’s AI strategy came into sharper focus as AWS-led spending plans highlighted the scale of its long-term cloud ambitions.

items.title: Beta Stock Soars 25%. Thank Amazon.
items.description: Amazon owns more than 5% of Beta Technologies’ stock. Amazon invested in 2021 out of its Climate Pledge Fund.

items.title: Walmart Over Amazon: Retailer Wins EBITDA Multiple Foot Race
items.description: Walmart still lags behind its online rival in market cap, even with a $1 trillion valuation, but makes up for it with its EV/EBITDA multiple.

items.title: Gene Munster Reckons AI Is In its 2nd Innings Not 3rd, Says Wall Street Misreading Big Tech Spending As Likes Of Meta And Google Pour Billions
items.description: Gene Munster said Wall Street is misreading Big Tech's massive AI spending, arguing that artificial intelligence is still in an early stage and nearing a real-world utility breakthrough even as Meta, Amazon and Alphabet face near-term stock pressure from elevated capital expenditures.
